stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Photogrammetry is the process of measuring real-world distances and shapes from overlapping photographs.
  • This process enables researchers to construct accurate three-dimensional models from sets of two-dimensional images.
  • A 2019 project by Adam Lowe and colleagues used photogrammetry to digitally reconstruct the tomb of Tutankhamun in Egypt.
  • The resulting model was used to build a full-scale replica of the tomb.
  • The replica has been displayed to the public, helping protect the original tomb from heavy visitor traffic.
stem: The student wants to define the term “photogrammetry.” Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: A 2019 project by Adam Lowe and colleagues digitally reconstructed the tomb of Tutankhamun and also used photogrammetry.
Photogrammetry is the process of measuring real-world distances and shapes from overlapping photographs in order to build accurate three-dimensional models from two-dimensional images.
A photogrammetry-based replica of Tutankhamun's tomb helps protect the original from heavy visitor traffic.
Adam Lowe and colleagues used photogrammetry in their reconstruction of the tomb of Tutankhamun.

rationale: Choice B is the best answer because it defines photogrammetry as the process of building three-dimensional models from overlapping two-dimensional photographs. Choice A is incorrect because it discusses Lowe's project, not the definition of the term. Choice C is incorrect because it describes a use case, not the definition. Choice D is incorrect because it states only that the team used photogrammetry, without defining it.
